info.name: BetaZen CDN — File Manager API
info.description: Multi-tenant file manager API. Auth: send `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` (an API key `fmsk_…` or a session/3rd-party JWT). Set the `baseUrl` and `token` collection variables, then run any request. Generated from the live API catalog.

item.name: Buckets & Folders
item.description: Containers for files. Every query is scoped to your vendor.

request.description:
All buckets for the current vendor.

API-key scope required: bucket:read

Create a new bucket. Names are unique per vendor.

API-key scope required: bucket:create

Body fields:
- `name` (string, required) — Bucket name, unique per vendor. Lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, must start with a letter or digit (^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}$). A matching folder is created at /var/www/vendors/<username>/<name>/.
- `description` (string, optional) — Optional, ≤ 500 chars.
- `isPublic` (boolean, optional) — Optional. Marks the bucket public. Default false.
- `settings.allowedMimeTypes` (string[], optional) — Optional allow-list of MIME types. Empty/omitted = any type allowed.
- `settings.maxFileSizeBytes` (integer, optional) — Optional per-file size cap in bytes. 0 or omitted = no per-bucket cap (the plan/global cap still applies).

Fetch one bucket by id.

API-key scope required: bucket:read

Rename or change settings.

API-key scope required: bucket:update

Body fields:
- `name` (string, optional) — New name (same rules as create). All fields are optional — send only what you want to change.
- `description` (string, optional) — Optional, ≤ 500 chars.
- `isPublic` (boolean, optional) — Optional.
- `settings.allowedMimeTypes` (string[], optional) — Optional. Replaces the existing allow-list.
- `settings.maxFileSizeBytes` (integer, optional) — Optional. 0 = no cap.

Delete a bucket. It must be empty.

API-key scope required: bucket:delete

Folders in a bucket. Pass showHidden=true to include hidden folders.

API-key scope required: file:list

Create a folder (optionally nested via parentId).

API-key scope required: folder:create

Body fields:
- `name` (string, required) — Folder name, 1–255 chars. Unique within its parent.
- `parentId` (string | null, optional) — Parent folder id to nest under. null or omitted = bucket root.

Rename a folder.

API-key scope required: folder:update

Body fields:
- `name` (string, required) — New folder name, 1–255 chars.

Delete a folder. It must be empty.

API-key scope required: folder:update

Move a folder under a new parent (same bucket). Descendant paths are updated automatically.

API-key scope required: folder:update

Body fields:
- `parentId` (string | null, required) — Target parent folder id (must be in the SAME bucket). null = move to the bucket root. Descendant paths re-compute automatically.

Hide a folder (and exclude it from default listings).

API-key scope required: folder:hide

Reverse a previous folder hide.

API-key scope required: folder:hide

item.name: Files
item.description: Upload, fetch, update and download files. Uploads are multipart/form-data.

request.description:
List files in a bucket. Filter by folderId or a text query q.

API-key scope required: file:list

Upload a file (multipart/form-data, field "file"). Optional fields: "path" — the destination folder inside the bucket (default "/"); "folderId", "tags", "metadata". The file is also written to your File Manager at /<bucket>/<path>/<filename>, so it shows up there. Returns the file id, full details, and serverPath.

API-key scope required: file:upload

Form fields (multipart/form-data):
- `file` (file, required) — The file bytes (multipart/form-data field name "file").
- `path` (string, optional) — Server sub-folder inside the bucket for the File Manager mirror. Default "/". The file is written to /<bucket>/<path>/<filename> so it appears in the File Manager.
- `folderId` (string | null, optional) — Object-storage folder to file it under. null/omitted = bucket root. Independent of "path".
- `tags` (string, optional) — Optional comma-separated tags.
- `metadata` (json string, optional) — Optional JSON object (as a string) of custom metadata.

formdata.description: Server sub-folder inside the bucket for the File Manager mirror. Default "/". The file is written to /<bucket>/<path>/<filename> so it appears in the File Manager. [optional]
Object-storage folder to file it under. null/omitted = bucket root. Independent of "path". [optional]
Optional JSON object (as a string) of custom metadata. [optional]

request.description:
Full metadata for a file (size, mime, checksum, tags, …).

API-key scope required: file:read

Rename or set tags / metadata.

API-key scope required: file:upload

Body fields:
- `originalName` (string, optional) — Rename the file (1–255). All fields optional — send only what changes.
- `tags` (string[], optional) — Replace the tag list.
- `metadata` (object, optional) — Replace custom metadata.
- `folderId` (string | null, optional) — Move the file to a folder in the SAME bucket. null = bucket root.

Soft-delete (move to trash). Restore with /files/:id/restore.

API-key scope required: file:delete

Streams the file bytes as an attachment through the app (Content-Disposition: attachment). The object store is internal, so the app proxies the bytes rather than redirecting.

API-key scope required: file:download

Restore a trashed file back to "ready". Re-checks quota and suspension.

API-key scope required: file:delete

Duplicate a file within the same bucket (optionally into a folder, with a new name). Storage-cheap — the underlying object is shared (de-duplicated), so the copy is instant.

API-key scope required: file:upload

Body fields:
- `folderId` (string | null, optional) — Destination folder (same bucket). null/omitted = bucket root.
- `name` (string, optional) — Name for the copy. Default "Copy of <original>".

Return the UTF-8 contents of a text file (≤ 1 MB) for inline editing.

API-key scope required: file:read

Overwrite a text file with new contents (≤ 5 MB). Writes a new version of the object and reclaims the old one. Enforces the per-file size cap.

API-key scope required: file:upload

Body fields:
- `content` (string, required) — New UTF-8 text content, ≤ 5 MB. The file must be textual; the per-file size cap is enforced.

Hide a file from default listings (still accessible by id). Use showHidden=true to list it.

API-key scope required: folder:hide

Reverse a previous hide.

API-key scope required: folder:hide

Unzip an uploaded archive into the bucket, recreating its folder structure. Guards against zip-bombs (≤ 2 GB uncompressed) and path traversal. Optionally extract into a target folder.

API-key scope required: file:upload

Body fields:
- `folderId` (string | null, optional) — Folder to extract the archive into. null/omitted = bucket root. Guards: ≤ 500 MB zip, ≤ 2 GB uncompressed, no path traversal.

Create a new file from inline text content (≤ 5 MB) without a multipart upload — handy for notes, configs, READMEs. Optional "path" sets the server folder inside the bucket (default "/"); the file also appears in your File Manager.

API-key scope required: file:upload

Body fields:
- `name` (string, required) — File name, 1–255 chars.
- `content` (string, optional) — Inline UTF-8 text, ≤ 5 MB. Default empty.
- `path` (string, optional) — Server sub-folder inside the bucket for the File Manager mirror. Default "/".
- `mimeType` (string, optional) — Optional content type. Inferred from the name when omitted.
- `folderId` (string | null, optional) — Object-storage folder. null/omitted = bucket root.

item.name: POST Zip files into an archive

request.description:
Create a .zip from selected files and/or folders and store it back in the bucket. Total source ≤ 1 GB.

API-key scope required: file:read

Body fields:
- `name` (string, optional) — Name for the produced .zip. Default auto-generated.
- `fileIds` (string[], at least one of fileIds / folderIds) — Files to include (≤ 2000).
- `folderIds` (string[], at least one of fileIds / folderIds) — Folders to include recursively (≤ 200).
- `folderId` (string | null, optional) — Where to store the resulting .zip. null = bucket root. Total source ≤ 1 GB.

item.name: Large uploads (multipart)
item.description: For big files, upload in parts: init → upload each part → complete. Abort to cancel.

request.description:
Begin a multipart upload. Returns an uploadId + storage key. Quota and per-file/bucket size limits are checked here and again on complete.

API-key scope required: file:upload

Body fields:
- `bucketId` (string, required) — 24-hex id of the destination bucket.
- `originalName` (string, required) — File name, 1–255 chars.
- `mimeType` (string, required) — Content type of the file.
- `sizeBytes` (integer, optional) — Optional declared size — quota and per-file/bucket caps are checked here (and authoritatively again on complete).
- `folderId` (string | null, optional) — Optional destination folder. null = bucket root.

Upload part number :n (1–10000) as the raw request body. Returns the part ETag — collect these for "complete".

API-key scope required: file:upload

Finalize the object from its uploaded parts. The server reads the authoritative stored size (HeadObject) and re-checks quota/limits before marking the file ready.

API-key scope required: file:upload

Body fields:
- `parts` ({ PartNumber, ETag }[], required) — Every uploaded part, from each "Upload a part" response. Order matters.
- `sizeBytes` (integer, optional) — Ignored if sent — the server reads the authoritative size from storage (HeadObject).

Cancel an in-progress multipart upload and release its uploaded parts.

API-key scope required: file:upload

item.name: Links
item.description: Three shareable link types: public, temporary (auto-expiring), private (JWT-gated).

request.description:
Active links for a file.

API-key scope required: file:read

Create a shareable link for a file. Three types: public (/p/), temporary (/t/, auto-expiring) and private (/d/, JWT-gated). Temporary links REQUIRE an explicit expiresIn and cannot be permanent; public/private links can be made permanent with neverExpire (otherwise, with no expiresIn, they default to 60 seconds). See the body fields below for every rule.

API-key scope required: publicurl:create

Body fields:
- `type` ('public' | 'temporary' | 'private', required) — public → /p/<token> (anyone with the URL). temporary → /t/<token> (auto-expires). private → /d/<token> (needs a 3rd-party JWT).
- `expiresIn` (integer (seconds), required for temporary) — Lifetime, 60 … 315,360,000 (10 years). TEMPORARY links must set it. For public/private it is optional — but a link with NO expiresIn and NO neverExpire defaults to just 60 seconds.
- `neverExpire` (boolean, not allowed for temporary) — Make a public/private link permanent. Rejected (400) for temporary links.
- `maxDownloads` (integer | null, optional) — Cap the total number of downloads; returns 410 once reached. Default unlimited.
- `password` (string, optional) — Optional, 4–100 chars. The downloader must send it via the x-link-password header (never the query string).
- `requiredScope` (string, optional) — PRIVATE links only — the JWT scope the caller must hold. Default "file:download".

Revoke every active link for the file. Optionally regenerate fresh ones.

API-key scope required: publicurl:revoke

Body fields:
- `types` (('public'|'temporary'|'private')[], optional) — Only revoke these link types. Omit = revoke all active links for the file.
- `regenerate` (boolean, optional) — If true, create fresh links of the revoked types after revoking. Default false.

Revoke a single link by id.

API-key scope required: publicurl:revoke

item.name: Realtime events
item.description: A live per-vendor activity feed (Server-Sent Events) so 3rd-party software gets pushed updates instead of polling — plus a JSON delta for fast catch-up.

request.description:
Realtime per-vendor event feed. DEFAULT: a Server-Sent Events stream (Content-Type: text/event-stream) — open it with a streaming HTTP client sending "Authorization: Bearer fmsk_…" and receive events ({id,type,vendorId,resourceType,resourceId,bucketId,at}) the instant they happen (file.upload, file.delete, link.create, bucket.*, fs.*, …). Auto-resume with the Last-Event-ID header. DELTA: add ?since=<cursor> for a one-shot JSON list of events after that cursor (poll using the returned cursor) — ideal for a fast initial load; omit ?since to get the most recent page. Bucket-scoped keys only receive events for their own buckets. Use the live "Try it" below to open the stream in your browser, or ?since for the JSON delta.

API-key scope required: events:subscribe

Also available over WebSocket (same events, same API key): wss://<host>/api/v1/ws — see the Integration Guide.

Event payload example:
{
  "id": "6a2e7012c12f65f87232a1f3",
  "type": "file.upload",
  "vendorId": "6a2d7e0a626116c181d92a71",
  "resourceType": "file",
  "resourceId": "6a2e70b4c12f65f87232a201",
  "bucketId": "6a2e6fc6c12f65f87232a172",
  "actorType": "apikey",
  "at": "2026-06-14T09:10:42.615Z"
}

Event types (the `type` field):
• Files: file.upload, file.create, file.update, file.edit, file.copy, file.delete, file.restore, file.download, file.hide, file.unhide, file.zip, file.extract, file.multipart.init, file.multipart.complete, file.multipart.abort
• Buckets: bucket.create, bucket.update, bucket.delete
• Folders: folder.create, folder.update, folder.delete, folder.hide, folder.unhide
• Share links: link.create, link.reset, link.revoke, link.download.public, link.download.temporary, link.download.private
• File manager (jailed FS): fs.mkdir, fs.newfile, fs.write, fs.upload, fs.rename, fs.copy, fs.delete, fs.trash, fs.restore, fs.chmod, fs.zip, fs.extract, fs.hide, fs.unhide

query.description: JSON delta: return events AFTER this cursor id. Omit for the live SSE stream.
SSE only: resume after this event id (or send the Last-Event-ID header).

item.name: File manager (your private folder)
item.description: A private, jailed filesystem area per vendor. Every path is relative to your home folder — you can never reach a parent, the server root, or another vendor.

request.description: List the contents of a path inside your private folder. Defaults to the root ("/").

Run a filesystem action: mkdir, newfile, write, rename, delete (permanent), copy, chmod, zip, extract, hide/unhide, or the recoverable trash actions (trash, restore, trash-purge, trash-empty). All paths are confined to your folder. List the trash with GET /fs?trash=1.

Body fields:
- `action` ('mkdir'|'newfile'|'write'|'rename'|'delete'|'copy'|'chmod'|'zip'|'extract'|'hide'|'unhide'|'trash'|'restore'|'trash-purge'|'trash-empty', required) — The filesystem operation. delete = permanent; trash = recoverable (moves to .trash); restore/trash-purge target a trash id; hide/unhide toggle a leading-dot name.
- `path` (string, most actions (not restore/trash-purge/trash-empty)) — Target path, relative to your private folder (cannot escape it). Defaults to "/".
- `to` (string, rename · copy · zip · extract) — Destination path for move/copy/zip/extract.
- `content` (string, write · newfile) — File contents for write/newfile (≤ 2 MB).
- `mode` (string, chmod) — Octal permission string, e.g. "644".
- `paths` (string[], zip) — Items to include in the archive (≤ 2000).
- `id` (string, restore · trash-purge) — A trash entry id (from GET /fs?trash=1) to restore or permanently purge.
Return the UTF-8 contents of a text file (≤ 2 MB) inside your folder.
Stream any file from your folder as an attachment.

Upload a file (multipart/form-data) into a directory of your private folder. Fields: "dir" + "file".

Form fields (multipart/form-data):
- `file` (file, required) — The upload (multipart/form-data field "file").
- `path` (string, optional) — Full destination path (relative; may include sub-folders). Takes precedence over "dir".
- `dir` (string, optional) — Destination directory (relative). Default "/".

formdata.description: Full destination path (relative; may include sub-folders). Takes precedence over "dir". [optional]

item.name: Shareable download URLs
item.description: Public-facing URLs returned by "Create link". These live at the site root (NOT under /api/v1) and need no API key — open them in a browser. Private links require a 3rd-party JWT.

request.description: Open a public link. Streams the file inline through the app (so it previews in the browser). Base = site root, e.g. https://cdn.betazeninfotech.com/p/:token . Append ?raw=1 to force the raw bytes; send x-link-password if the link is password-protected.
Open a time-limited link; streams the file inline. Returns 410 once expired or once maxDownloads is reached. Send x-link-password if the link is password-protected.
Open a private link. Send Authorization: Bearer <3rd-party JWT> with the matching scope (and bucket scope) for the file.
